items.title: Whyalla locals react to news of steelworks' final bidders

children.content: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.

articles.title: Two remaining Whyalla steelworks bidders announced, BlueScope retains last offer right

children.content: Peter Malinauskas has announced M Resources and Jindal Steel are the two bidders remaining for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks, while BlueScope Steel retains its right of last offer.

image.altText: A large smelter or factory with dust and smoke around

gallery.altText: A man wearing a blue hat, top and jeans sitting on a pipe next to a road

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a blue hat, top and jeans sitting on a pipe next to a road

items.title: Original track designer steers clear of estimated $100m MotoGP remodel

children.content: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

image.altText: A narrow bitumen road runs between trees and large patches of grass

items.title: Domestic violence operation results in 70 arrests in SA

children.content: SA Police say they targeted 351 offenders as part of Operation Storm Days of Action, attending 443 addresses across the state in April.

image.altText: Crime scene tape outside a home.

items.title: Murderer convicted of 1999 crime back in custody for breaching parole

children.content: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.

image.altText: The exterior of the Supreme Court of South Australia.

items.title: Outback school finally set to be rebuilt after mould forced demolition

children.content: More than two years since a health hazard led to Broken Hill's Willyama High School being knocked down, work on the "world-class" facility planned to replace is expected to begin.

image.altText: Five people in high-vis vests and hard hats with shovels of dirt at a construction site.

items.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont, police say

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

articles.title: SA Police return to Yunta as part of Gus Lamont investigation

children.content: Police have returned to Yunta in South Australia's far north as part of their investigation into the disappearance of four-year-old boy Gus Lamont.

image.altText: Gus Lamont

articles.title: Where is Gus? Six months on, more questions than answers remain

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

image.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: Young farmer who died two weeks after engagement set to leave lasting legacy

children.content: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

image.altText: Man and woman wearing cowboy hats, sitting on a log

items.title: The extraordinary legacy of a trailblazing female driving instructor

children.content: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.

image.altText: Black and white photo from newspaper showing woman speaking into a microphone

items.title: Police officer accused of rape denied he was 'sleazy', court told

children.content: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

image.altText: An old stone building with a red dome

items.title: SAPOL commissioner rejects outback police shortage plan

children.content: Commissioner Grant Stevens rejects recommendations to attract more officers to the understaffed Coober Pedy station over funding concerns.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Coober Pedy

items.title: Archibald Prize sitter calls out APY art collective mistreatment

children.content: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

image.altText: A seated Aboriginal woman holds a microphone and gestures with her free hand.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Late pastoralist's family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for bushfire

children.content: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

image.altText: Smoke about the Lucindale grass fire

items.title: 'A devastating mess': Owner laments destruction and theft of organ pipes at former church

children.content: The pipe theft comes days after a bronze statue was stolen from the centre of a fountain in Adelaide's inner north-eastern suburbs.

gallery.altText: A man standing in a darkened church with organ keys in the foreground

thumbnail.altText: A man standing in a darkened church with organ keys in the foreground

items.title: Flights resume at training school after two killed in plane crash

children.content: An aviation school resumes flight operations at Adelaide's Parafield Airport four weeks after an instructor and a student onboard a twin-engine plane were killed when it crashed into a hangar.

image.altText: Black smoke billows from a plane crash at Parafield Airport

items.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows fast bowling can take a toll

children.content: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

image.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

items.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work

children.content: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.

items.title: Headline inflation eases to 4.2 per cent in April as fuel prices fall

children.content: Headline inflation decelerated in April, with consumer prices increasing at an annual pace of 4.2 per cent, down from 4.6 per cent in March.

image.altText: photo of a hand with empty cup of coffee and cash on a wooden table

items.title: ASX jumps as odds of June rate rise move close to zero — as it happened

children.content: Shares climbed on the ASX after softer than expected inflation figures sent market bets of a June rate rise to less than 10 per cent.

image.altText: Business people walk past a shiny black sign that reads "Reserve Bank of Australia".

items.title: This 24yo started gambling as a teenager and has now lost 900k

children.content: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teenagers gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

items.title: Media leaks ahead of Ben Roberts-Smith arrest referred to corruption commission

children.content: News crews were at Sydney Airport as a plane carrying Ben Roberts-Smith landed, and captured images of his arrest from inside the terminal.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith, face blurred, exits plane onto Sydney Airport tarmac flanked by officers and men in suits.

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

image.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

items.title: More than a million worshippers brave searing heat for annual Hajj pilgrimage

children.content: Thousands of Muslim worshippers converge on the rocky hills and plains of Saudi Arabia's Mount Arafat as the annual Islamic pilgrimage, the Hajj, reached its peak.

items.title: Australian travellers urged to check measles immunity

children.content: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

image.altText: A vial of the measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR) vaccine is pictured

items.title: Bystanders foil alleged kidnap attempt of woman changing tyre in Sydney

children.content: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

image.altText: A man in a tracksuit is handcuffed while surrounded by several NSW Police officers.

items.title: Cuts of meat that will give you more bang for your buck

children.content: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: Police search for missing swimmer in WA's South West

children.content: Police say the woman entered the water at Koombana Bay just after 9:20am and has not been seen since

image.altText: Police standing at the shoreline of Koombana Bay

items.title: Booming cockroach numbers welcome news on this 'paradise of creatures'

children.content: Researchers say the removal of invasive rodents on Lord Howe Island has sparked a rebound of invertebrate life, including native cockroaches and other insects, vital to the broader ecosystem.

items.title: Court increases jail sentence for remorseless sex worker killer

children.content: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.

image.altText: Xiaozheng Lin stands against a grey backdrop in a police mug shot.

items.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?

children.content: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

image.altText: A drone image of a block of land with bushland on surrounded by buildings, road and the beach.

items.title: Teaching student charged with online child abuse offences

children.content: A Victorian teaching student, who had recently worked as a childcare centre cook, a foster carer
and was a school council president, is facing charges relating to online child abuse material.

image.altText: A man with glasses smiles at the camera.

items.title: Several cars destroyed after blaze at Queensland tyre shop

children.content: Emergency services were called to the business on Queens Road in Kingston just after 1pm, with more than a dozen fire department crews on the scene.

items.title: Concussion saw Macy lose her 'spark', but a new trial helped her recover

children.content: Macy became a shell of her usual self after suffering a concussion on a school trip, but experienced a huge improvement after a trial designed to help children.
